title: Define an Application Shell
text: Define an Application Shell An application shell is required to run Windows and character-based applications. You can also define a shell for customized application server. You must have created a project to contain your application definitions. For more information, see Working with Projects . To define an application shell: In the U-Bar, click Browse, select prj , then choose the project you want to open. In the Templates tab of the Resource Browser, select the template for the type of application shell you want to create. Drag and drop onto the project object in the Structure. Right-click the new application shell and click Open to open it in the Application Shell Editor. Notice that the Shell Type property indicates the type of application shell. The value of this property determines the other properties and triggers, if any, that can be edited for the shell. Set the properties applicable for your application shell. In More Properties , click the More icon () to open the Define Application Shell Properties dialog. Edit the properties of the application shell as required, then click OK . In the script editor, write ProcScript to define the application environment and actions required to initialize the application. Click Compile to compile the application shell. Related Links Define an Application Server Shell Create a an application server shell when you want to define customized behavior for a Uniface server. Define a Windows Application Shell Create a Windows shell for Uniface applications that run on any supported Windows platform. Define a Character Mode Application Shell Create a shell for Uniface applications that run in character mode on any supported platform, such as Unix or Linux. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
